Paragraph

But if we consider this linear map as from \({\mathcal R}[-\pi, \pi]\) to \(l^{2}\text{,}\) it does not have a bounded norm if we equip function a \(f\in {\mathcal R}[-\pi, \pi]\) with the norm \(\int_{-\pi}^{\pi}|f(x)|\, dx\text{,}\) as for any \(C>0\text{,}\) there exists \(f\in {\mathcal R}[-\pi, \pi]\text{,}\) such that
\begin{equation*} \Vert (a_{0}, a_{1}, b_{1}, \ldots) \Vert_{l^2}\ge \left( \frac{1}{2\pi} \int_{-\pi}^{\pi}|f(x)|^{2}\;dx\right)^{1/2}\ge C \int_{-\pi}^{\pi}|f(x)|\;dx. \end{equation*}
in-context
